preview_text:
The model backs Germany to win at 57%, with Paraguay's chance standing at 15%. This represents a moderate favorite rather than a dominant one; the model assigns meaningful probability to an upset, reflecting competitive uncertainty despite the gap in win chances.

The 28% draw probability shapes the match as genuinely competitive. A draw is nearly as likely as a Paraguay win outright, suggesting close scorelines are plausible. The spread across outcomes—no single result exceeding 57%—indicates the model expects a closely contested encounter without a clear consensus outcome.

The market diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks price Germany's win at 72%, roughly 15 percentage points higher than the model, while pricing Paraguay at 10% rather than 15%. Polymarket prices align closely with the books. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the gap suggests material disagreement on Germany's edge in this fixture.
